name: 2026-06-07 Constitutional Awareness FOI Bill Constitution Catching Up TH
description:
39 years.

That's how long the 1987 Constitution has said it: 'full public disclosure of all its transactions involving public interest.' Article II, Section 28.

The Senate passed a Freedom of Information bill last month. The press is calling it progress.

It is not progress. It is the country catching up to its own Constitution.

Apat na dekada para sa karapatan na atin na pala.

custom_fields.value:
39 years.

That's how long the 1987 Constitution has said it: 'full public disclosure of all its transactions involving public interest.' Article II, Section 28.

The Senate passed a Freedom of Information bill last month. The press is calling it progress.

It is not progress. It is the country catching up to its own Constitution.

Apat na dekada para sa karapatan na atin na pala.

39 years.

That's how long the 1987 Constitution has said it: 'full public disclosure of all its transactions involving public interest.' Article II, Section 28.

The Senate passed a Freedom of Information bill last month. The press is calling it progress.

It is not progress. It is the country catching up to its own Constitution.

Apat na dekada para sa karapatan na atin na pala.

Senate passed SB 1432 People's FOI Act on third reading May 4-5, 2026 (Manila Times, Manila Bulletin). House Right to Information Act at committee level (Rappler, Open Government Partnership tracker). LEDAC set June 2026 deadline. Bicam conference still pending. Underlying constitutional anchor: Article II Section 28 of the 1987 Constitution has adopted a policy of full public disclosure of all its transactions involving public interest since ratification on February 2, 1987 (Official Gazette). Current FOI in PH is only Executive Order 2 s.2016 covering the executive branch; no statutory law covers Congress, Judiciary, or LGUs. Ambient context: DOF Sec. Recto estimated PHP 42.3B-118.5B in economic losses 2023-2025 from ghost flood control projects (GMA, BusinessWorld, Inquirer) — the kind of cost a working FOI statute would surface earlier.
